title: Display and report generation platform for testing results
abstract: A data collection, display, and report generation platform has a first input interface configured to present a learning module comprising a series of questions and answers on a plurality of successive question/answer screens comprising a plurality of radio buttons, at least one of the radio buttons configured to accept both a first input action and a subsequent second input action, each providing a different visual indication. The first and second input action each indicate a different confidence level of a learner's answer. A display dashboard displays data visualizations of metrics of misinformation and struggle of plurality of learners based on a plurality of answers collected through the first input interface, and comprises one or more bar graph displays, one or more heat map displays, and one or more sorting tools configured to alter the one or more bar graph displays or one or more heat map displays.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed is: 1. A data collection, display, and report generation platform comprising: a first input interface configured to: present a learning module comprising a series of questions and answers on a plurality of successive question/answer screens of the first input interface, wherein at least one of the successive question/answer screens are presented based on answers on a previous question/answer screen, and at least one of the plurality of successive screens comprising a plurality of radio buttons, each of the plurality of radio buttons associated with an answer to a question, and at least one of the radio buttons configured to accept both: a first input action alone, and the first input action and a subsequent second input action, wherein the first input action causes the at least one of the radio buttons to provide a first visual indication of the first input action, and the subsequent second input action causes the at least one of the radio buttons to provide a second visual indication of the second input action, wherein the first input action and the second input action each indicate a different confidence level of a userâ s answer; a display dashboard configured to: display a plurality of data visualizations of metrics of misinformation and struggle of plurality of users on one or more interactive screens based on a plurality of answers collected through the first input interface, wherein the display dashboard comprises: one or more bar graph displays; 

 one or more heat map displays; and one or more sorting tools configured to alter the one or more bar graph displays or one or more heat map displays. 2. The platform of claim 1, further comprising: a report generation interface configured to display a list comprising one or more learners and at least one answer sequence provided by the one or more learners, wherein the answer sequence: was collected from answers entered on the first input interface by the one or more learners, and indicates the metrics of misinformation or struggle by the one or more learners. 3. The platform of claim 1, wherein the display dashboard comprises: a course misinformation dashboard; and a course struggle dashboard. 4. The platform of claim 1, wherein the one or more heat map displays are sortable by learner and topic. 5. The platform of claim 1, where the one or more bar graph displays are sortable by learner and topic. 6. The platform of claim 1, wherein the one or more heat map displays are sortable by each of hire date, geographic region, functional region, and performance level. 7. The platform of claim 1, wherein the one or more bar graph displays are sortable by each of hire date, geographic region, functional region, and performance level. 8. The platform of claim 4, wherein the one or more heat map displays comprise a plurality of color-coded blocks each corresponding to percentages of misinformation or struggle for each learner on each topic. 9. The platform of claim 8, wherein the one or more heat map displays further comprise: a selector tool that corresponds to ranges of percentages and the adjustment of which hides at least one of the color-coded blocks. 10. The platform of claim 4, wherein each of the color-coded blocks of the heat map display are configured to show a pop-up text and numerical display of information when a cursor is held over any of the color-coded blocks. 11. The platform of claim 10, wherein the text and numerical display comprise one or more of: a name of a learner; a description of a topic; and a numerical percentage. 12. The platform of claim 4, wherein the heat map display is configured to highlight a name and a topic corresponding to each of the color coded blocks when a cursor is held over any of the color-coded blocks 13. The platform of claim 1, wherein the first input interface is configured to display visual prompts of an artificial-intelligence guidance system, 

 wherein the prompts comprise written instructions or encouragement to a user and appear in response to particular answer sequences. 14. The platform of claim 1, wherein the first input action causes a written description of the confidence to appear adjacent to the radio button. 15. A data collection, display, and report generation platform comprising: a first input interface configured to: present a series of questions and answers on a plurality of successive question/answer screens of the first input interface, wherein at least one of the successive question/answer screens are presented based on answers on a previous question/answer screen, and at least one of the plurality of successive screens comprising a plurality of radio buttons, each of the plurality of radio buttons associated with an answer to a question, and at least one of the radio buttons configured to accept both: a first input action alone, and the first input action and a subsequent second input action, wherein the first input action causes the at least one of the radio buttons to provide a first visual indication of the first input action, and the subsequent second input action causes the at least one of the radio buttons to provide a second visual indication of the second input action, wherein the first input action and the second input action each indicate a different confidence level of a learnerâ s answer; a learner dashboard configured to: display a plurality of data visualizations of metrics of module and course progress of the learner on one or more interactive screens based on a 

 plurality of answers collected through the first input interface, wherein the learner dashboard is configured to display: a first circular graph indicating module progress; a second circular graph indicating course progress; a time-spent bar graph comparing time spent by a learner to other learners; and an estimated time of completion. 16. The platform of claim 15, further comprising: a refresh and review interface comprising each of: a smart refresh module configured to provide a retake version of the learning module that omits any questions answered confidently and correctly upon first presentation to the learner in the learning module; a full refresh module configured to provide a full retake of the learning module; a review list comprising all questions, answers, and learning materials from the learning module. 17. The platform of claim 15, wherein the first input interface comprises a progress bar that fills in visual indicators of progress via a first marker indicating mastery, a second progress marker indicating uncertainty, and a third progress marker indicating misinformation. 18. The platform of claim 15, wherein the first input action causes a written description of the confidence to appear adjacent to the radio button. 19. The platform of claim 15, wherein the first input interface is configured to display visual prompts of an artificial-intelligence guidance system, wherein the prompts comprise written instructions or encouragement to a learner and appear in response to particular answer sequences. 20. The platform of claim 19, wherein the visual prompts of the artificial- intelligence guidance system instruct a learner to indicate the learnerâs confidence level accurately in response to an answer sequence indicating cheating.
